title: BU signs $104M PILOT agreement with city, the largest in Boston history
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News This is BU's first PILOT agreement in more than 25 years.
People walk through Boston University's campus near Marsh Chapel.
Jessica Rinaldi/The Boston Globe Boston University and the city of Boston have finalized the largest payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (PILOT) agreement in the city’s history, officials said.

title: Logan Airport sees hundreds of delays and cancellations due to thunderstorms
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Transportation More than 300 flights were canceled and more than 400 flights were delayed, with rain expected to continue throughout the night.
JetBlue and Avianca planes sit at Terminal E gates at Boston Logan International Airport.
Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff Boston Logan International Airport is seeing hundreds of delays and cancellations Tuesday night amid a thunderstorm.

title: Cape Cod influencer sues, says she was barred from JD Vance event because of social media posts mocking VP
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News "The government simply can't retaliate against you for your political viewpoint.
" JD Vance delivers remarks on the Trump administration's anti-fraud initiatives in Milwaukee on July 8.

Scott Olson/Getty Images The ACLU of Maine is suing the Trump administration, alleging federal officials violated the First Amendment by barring a local political influencer known for mocking Vice President JD Vance online from attending one of his events.

title: These 9 statewide ballot questions will be up for a vote in November
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Politics The ballot measures range from adjusting the cap on state tax revenue and modifying the state's voting and election procedures to overturning previous legislation related to firearms and marijuana.
Secretary of the Commonwealth William Galvin holds ballots for the 2024 election.
Pat Greenhouse/The Boston Globe Massachusetts voters will face nine statewide ballot questions when they head to the polls for the next statewide election.

title: Former employee at adult group home in Fall River charged over assault captured on video
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Bedline Belrice, 31, has since been terminated from her role as a caretaker at Crystal Springs, the residential center and special education school said.
A screenshot from the video apparently showing a former Crystal Springs employee assaulting a 33-year-old woman who was diagnosed with autism and is nonverbal.
Carlos Andrade Melo/Facebook An employee at a Fall River residential center and special education school is facing charges for the alleged assault of a disabled person that was captured on video.

title: Dorchester 13-year-old has been missing since Thursday, BPD says
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Local News Trabian Scantlebury, 13, was last seen July 16 in Dorchester, the Boston Police Department said.
Trabian Scantlebury, 13, was last seen in Dorchester on July 16, according to the Boston Police Department.
Via Boston Police Department By Audrey Cooney July 21, 2026 | 5:52 PM Less than a minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Ralph’s Coffee opens first permanent Boston cafe in the Seaport
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Food News The luxury fashion brand opened its first permanent Ralph's Coffee in Boston alongside a new Seaport clothing store as it expands the café concept worldwide.
The inside of the new Ralph's Coffee, a Ralph Lauren coffee shop, in the Seaport District in Boston.
Ralph Lauren/Handout Ralph Lauren is betting that some customers in Boston will discover the brand over a latte before buying a polo shirt.

title: ESPN pundit: Bill Belichick should seriously consider retirement before ‘pathetic’ ending at UNC
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots "He chose the wrong place.
He's tried to use the magic of yesteryear and it simply hasn't worked.
" North Carolina head coach Bill Belichick.
